headline: Section 124B sedition charges target online government critics
context: Penal Code Section 124B on activities detrimental to parliamentary democracy was used against 11 online government critics in 2024.

cards.big: 11 online critics charged under Section 124B of Penal Code in 2024
cards.sub: Vague 'activities detrimental to parliamentary democracy' provision weaponised

cards.big: Penal Code Section 124B carries up to 20 years imprisonment
cards.sub: Broad wording allows criminalisation of virtually any critical political expression

cards.big: UN Human Rights Committee condemned overbroad sedition provisions in 2024 review
cards.sub: Section 124B cited as incompatible with Article 19 ICCPR standards on expression

cards.big: 8 of 11 charged were social media users with fewer than 5,000 followers
cards.sub: Targeting ordinary citizens sends message that criticism is dangerous

cards.big: Charging critics for threatening democracy is itself the threat to democracy
Lawyers for Liberty represented 6 of 11 defendants pro bono in 2024
